channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft claims its servers were illegally accessed to make unsafe AI content
item.description: Unsurprisingly, a scheme which involved developing tools to steal API keys and access Microsoft servers without permission isn’t faring well in a Virginia court.
item.dc:creator: luke.hughes@futurenet.com (Luke Hughes), TechRadar

item.title: Reddit’s ad business is expanding—but it’s still small compared to other platforms
item.description: Reddit can’t stop building shiny new advertising tools. First it was dynamic product ads, then it was AI copy tools. Now, they’re premiering custom “Ask Me Anything”-themed ads.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: This cute convertible laptop looks like a mini PC with a touchscreen, a battery and a keyboard
item.description: Chuwi MiniBook X N150 combines tablet portability and notebook performance with a compact 10.5-inch touchscreen and a convertible 360-degree hinge design.
item.dc:creator: udinmwenefosa@gmail.com (Efosa Udinmwen), TechRadar

item.title: Lasers help archaeologists study ancient tattoos on Peruvian mummies
item.description: Researchers have used lasers to uncover highly intricate designs of ancient tattoos on mummies from Peru.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: AI experts tamp-down hype, project challenges for 2025
item.description: A trio of AI researchers and experts in Lithuania and England have tossed some cold water on the hype surrounding generative AI and predict that in 2025, companies and investors will have to lower their expectations regarding what the technology and large language models (LLMs) can do.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: The Making of a Bad Boy: Watch Diddy's Sordid Secrets Online
item.description: Watch Diddy: The Making of a Bad Boy online, the first documentary in a two-part series revealing the sordid secrets of the fallen music superstar.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Zuckerberg says Biden officials would 'scream,' 'curse' at Meta team over COVID takedown requests
item.description: Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg said Biden administration officials would “scream” and “curse” at his employees when they disagreed with the government’s takedown requests over pandemic-related content.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Prime Video's use of AI, Next Gen Stats on NFL games is helping viewers understand the game better
item.description: As Prime Video wraps up its third season doing NFL games with an AFC wild-card matchup between the Pittsburgh Steelers and Baltimore Ravens on Saturday night, the “Prime Vision” feed and use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the broadcasts continue to change the way football fans are able to analyz
item.dc:creator: Joe Reedy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Five takeaways from GSA’s latest Section 508 assessment
item.description: Agencies are dedicating more time and staff to digital accessibility efforts, but acquisition is still a key hurdle when it comes to Section 508 compliance. The post Five takeaways from GSA’s latest Section 508 assessment first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Ephemeral environments in cloud-native development
item.description: An emerging trend in cloud computing is using ephemeral environments for development and testing. Ephemeral environments are temporary, isolated spaces created for specific projects.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Rust 1.84 introduces strict provenance APIs
item.description: Rust 1.84 has been released, bringing strict provenance APIs that can be used in lieu of integer-pointer casts, making the code both easier for developers to reason about and easier for the compiler to analyze, the Rust team said.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Zuckerberg met with Trump before ending fact-checking program: GOP senator
item.description: Sen. Markwayne Mullin (R-Okla.) said Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg met with President-elect Trump a day ahead of announcing the social media network will eliminate its fact-checking program to prioritize free speech.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Uncertainty over Trump’s electric vehicle policies clouds 2025 forecast for carmakers
item.description: S&P Global Mobility expects global sales of 15.1 million battery electric vehicles in 2025, which would mark a 30% jump. Battery electric vehicles are expected to make up 16.7% of the market share for light vehicles.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Apple doubles down on privacy after Siri-snooping settlement
item.description: Apple has vehemently denied that it ever abused recordings of Siri requests by using those records for marketing, ad sales, or any of the other creepy nonsense we’re being forced to tolerate with other connected devices.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Meloni says Italy is exploring deals on telecoms security, but denies private talks with Musk
item.description: ROME (AP) — Italy’s Premier Giorgia Meloni said Thursday that her government is in talks with several private companies, including Elon Musk’s SpaceX, over the country’s telecoms security system, but denied having discussed the issue privately with Musk. “I never talked about this with Musk.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The ‘Worst in Show’ CES products put your data at risk and cause waste, privacy advocates say
item.description: So much of the technology showcased at CES includes gadgets made to improve consumers’ lives — whether by leveraging AI to make devices that help people become more efficient, by creating companions to cure loneliness or by providing tools that help people with mental and physical health.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Meta rolls back hate speech rules as Zuckerberg cites 'recent elections' as a catalyst
item.description: It wasn't just fact-checking that Meta scrapped from its platforms as it prepares for the second Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal cyber leaders on building national resilience
item.description: How are you building resilience into your agency’s cybersecurity? The post Federal cyber leaders on building national resilience first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Examining disk space on Linux
item.description: There are many ways to evaluate disk space usage on Linux, and this post examines a number of commands that allow you to view used and available disk space.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Intro to Ktor: The HTTP server for Kotlin
item.description: Kotlin is a modern general-purpose language that brings together object-oriented and functional programming power in a sleek syntax. Ktor is the official HTTP server for Kotlin, leveraging Kotlin’s expressiveness for building endpoints and other server-side necessities.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
